discrepancies.explanation: A prior abnormal result (an A1c of 9.8%) is on the chart and was raised by the patient, but the visit defers it with no follow-up plan.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: PT: Also — did that bloodwork from last time come back alright? I never heard anything.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Do not defer an A1c of 9.8%. Address the abnormal A1c today: repeat the test and/or adjust therapy, and document the plan.

discrepancies.explanation: A prior abnormal result (an INR of 4.6) is on the chart and was raised by the patient, but the visit defers it with no follow-up plan.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: PT: Also — did that bloodwork from last time come back alright? I never heard anything.

chart_evidence.display: INR in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay

discrepancies.suggested_fix: Do not defer an INR of 4.6. Address the abnormal INR today: repeat the test and/or adjust therapy, and document the plan.

discrepancies.explanation: Newly prescribed ibuprofen interacts with the patient's active lisinopril: kidney injury / reduced BP control.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: Let's add ibuprofen 600 mg three times daily — that should help.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Avoid co-prescribing ibuprofen with lisinopril (kidney injury / reduced BP control). Select a non-interacting alternative or adjust/monitor closely.

discrepancies.explanation: A prior abnormal result (an INR of 4.6) is on the chart and was raised by the patient, but the visit defers it with no follow-up plan.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: PT: Also — did that bloodwork from last time come back alright? I never heard anything.

chart_evidence.display: INR in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay

discrepancies.suggested_fix: Do not defer an INR of 4.6. Address the abnormal INR today: repeat the test and/or adjust therapy, and document the plan.

discrepancies.explanation: lisinopril is contraindicated in pregnancy (known teratogen) and the chart documents an active pregnancy.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: I'll start you on lisinopril 20 mg daily for that.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Do not prescribe lisinopril in pregnancy. Choose a pregnancy-safe alternative and counsel the patient.

discrepancies.explanation: The patient reports having stopped lisinopril, an active chart medication, and the plan does not restart or address it.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: PT: Oh — I actually ran out of the lisinopril a couple months ago and never refilled it.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Reconcile: lisinopril is still on the active problem/med list. Confirm whether to restart lisinopril (ACE inhibitor) and document the decision.

discrepancies.explanation: lisinopril is contraindicated in pregnancy (known teratogen) and the chart documents an active pregnancy.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: I'll start you on lisinopril 20 mg daily for that.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Do not prescribe lisinopril in pregnancy. Choose a pregnancy-safe alternative and counsel the patient.

adverse_events.explanation: Patient reports trouble sleeping, a recognized adverse effect of their active sertraline (SSRI); temporal onset after a medication change raises suspicion of an ADR.
adverse_events.transcript_evidence: PT: One new thing — for the last few weeks I've had trouble sleeping. It started after we changed my medicines.
adverse_events.suggested_fix: Consider sertraline as the cause of trouble sleeping. Evaluate for an adverse drug reaction; weigh dose reduction, switch, or rechallenge.

discrepancies.explanation: simvastatin and the active home med atorvastatin are both statins — duplicate therapy in the same class.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: I want to add simvastatin 20 mg nightly to get better control.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Avoid stacking two statins. Titrate the existing atorvastatin or switch, rather than adding simvastatin.

discrepancies.explanation: enalapril and the active home med lisinopril are both ACE inhibitors — duplicate therapy in the same class.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: I want to add enalapril 10 mg twice daily to get better control.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Avoid stacking two ACE inhibitors. Titrate the existing lisinopril or switch, rather than adding enalapril.

discrepancies.explanation: metformin started at standard dose but the most recent eGFR is 26 mL/min/1.73m2. Rule: contraindicated when eGFR < 30; do not start 30-45.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: I'll get you going on metformin 1000 mg twice daily for your blood sugar.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Hold or renally dose metformin; recheck renal function. At eGFR 26, contraindicated when eGFR < 30; do not start 30-45.

adverse_events.explanation: Patient reports palpitations, a recognized adverse effect of their active levothyroxine (thyroid hormone); temporal onset after a medication change raises suspicion of an ADR.
adverse_events.transcript_evidence: PT: One new thing — for the last few weeks I've had palpitations. It started after we changed my medicines.
adverse_events.suggested_fix: Consider levothyroxine as the cause of palpitations. Evaluate for an adverse drug reaction; weigh dose reduction, switch, or rechallenge.

adverse_events.explanation: Patient reports dizziness, a recognized adverse effect of their active hydrochlorothiazide (thiazide diuretic); temporal onset after a medication change raises suspicion of an ADR.
adverse_events.transcript_evidence: PT: One new thing — for the last few weeks I've had dizziness. It started after we changed my medicines.
adverse_events.suggested_fix: Consider hydrochlorothiazide as the cause of dizziness. Evaluate for an adverse drug reaction; weigh dose reduction, switch, or rechallenge.

discrepancies.explanation: amoxicillin was prescribed but the chart lists a documented Penicillin allergy; amoxicillin is an aminopenicillin and cross-reacts.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: For that I'm going to start you on amoxicillin 500 mg three times daily — should clear a chest infection up in about a week.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Do not prescribe amoxicillin. Choose a non-cross-reactive alternative (e.g. a macrolide or, for UTI, nitrofurantoin if renal function allows) and confirm the allergy with the patient.

discrepancies.explanation: Newly prescribed potassium chloride interacts with the patient's active lisinopril: hyperkalemia.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: Let's add potassium chloride 20 mEq daily — that should help.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Avoid co-prescribing potassium chloride with lisinopril (hyperkalemia). Select a non-interacting alternative or adjust/monitor closely.

discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: Let's add sildenafil 50 mg as needed — that should help.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Avoid co-prescribing sildenafil with isosorbide mononitrate (life-threatening hypotension). Select a non-interacting alternative or adjust/monitor closely.

discrepancies.explanation: sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim was prescribed but the chart lists a documented Sulfa drugs allergy; sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim is a sulfonamide antibiotic and cross-reacts.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: For that I'm going to start you on sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim 800/160 mg twice daily — should clear a urinary tract infection up in about a week.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Do not prescribe sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim. Choose a non-cross-reactive alternative (e.g. a macrolide or, for UTI, nitrofurantoin if renal function allows) and confirm the allergy with the patient.

discrepancies.explanation: The patient reports having stopped insulin glargine, an active chart medication, and the plan does not restart or address it.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: PT: Oh — I actually ran out of the insulin glargine a couple months ago and never refilled it.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Reconcile: insulin glargine is still on the active problem/med list. Confirm whether to restart insulin glargine (basal insulin) and document the decision.

discrepancies.explanation: amoxicillin was prescribed but the chart lists a documented Penicillin allergy; amoxicillin is an aminopenicillin and cross-reacts.
discrepancies.transcript_evidence: DR: For that I'm going to start you on amoxicillin 500 mg three times daily — should clear a chest infection up in about a week.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Do not prescribe amoxicillin. Choose a non-cross-reactive alternative (e.g. a macrolide or, for UTI, nitrofurantoin if renal function allows) and confirm the allergy with the patient.

adverse_events.explanation: Patient reports blood in my urine, a recognized adverse effect of their active warfarin (anticoagulant); temporal onset after a medication change raises suspicion of an ADR.
adverse_events.transcript_evidence: PT: One new thing — for the last few weeks I've had blood in my urine. It started after we changed my medicines.
adverse_events.suggested_fix: Consider warfarin as the cause of blood in my urine. Evaluate for an adverse drug reaction; weigh dose reduction, switch, or rechallenge.

discrepancies.transcript_evidence: PT: Oh — I actually ran out of the levothyroxine a couple months ago and never refilled it.
discrepancies.suggested_fix: Reconcile: levothyroxine is still on the active problem/med list. Confirm whether to restart levothyroxine (thyroid hormone) and document the decision.
